channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Iran warns U.S. of Hormuz ‘red line,’ says it will retaliate to Trump’s strike threats
item.description: Trump warned that the U.S. will target Iranian infrastructure next week if negotiations do not resume.

item.title: TSMC to invest additional $100 billion in Arizona after second-quarter profit soars 77%
item.description: TSMC announced a second-quarter profit, following its June revenue figures released earlier this week.

item.title: U.S. slaps 25% tariff on most Brazilian goods over 'unfair trade practices'
item.description: A separate U.S. probe into forced-labor enforcement could see an additional 12.5% duty on Brazilian goods on top of the 25%, with the decision due next week.

item.title: Ukrainians take to the streets after Zelenskyy ousts defense minister in surprise shake-up
item.description: Ukrainian protesters took to the streets of Kyiv on Thursday, decrying Zelenskyy's surprise decision to dismiss Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

item.title: What Netflix can say after earnings Thursday to get its mojo back
item.description: Wall Street is looking for a catalyst as sentiment around the dominant streaming platform stock is souring.

item.title: Trump's pick to head national intelligence, Jay Clayton, won't tell senators Biden won the 2020 election
item.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee is considering Jay Clayton's nomination to be the director of national intelligence.

item.title: United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
item.description: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.

item.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
item.description: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

item.title: Anthropic moves closer to mega-IPO as bankers line up investor meetings
item.description: Anthropic is lining up investor meetings ahead of a potential IPO as soon as October as the AI startup behind looks to beat rival OpenAI to the public markets.

item.title: $100 million New Jersey deli fraud defendant Patten wants no prison time despite past conviction
item.description: James Patten is the person to be sentenced for a scheme that sent the market capitalization of a company that owned just a small New Jersey deli soaring.

item.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
item.description: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

item.title: Public Service Loan Forgiveness has new rules — 3 changes borrowers should know about
item.description: Recent changes to Public Service Loan Forgiveness should prompt borrowers to confirm that their repayment plan and loan type remain eligible for the relief.

item.title: Jim Cramer says he needs 'cold hard' proof that AI is paying off
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said he’s looking for companies to start showing measurable financial returns from their AI investments.

item.title: Fed Chairman Warsh says he meets 'often' with Trump administration, defends independence
item.description: Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that he speaks with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent frequently outside their regular weekly meetings.

item.title: Trump blasts New York AI data center moratorium, says state should change policy 'immediately'
item.description: New York became the first state in the U.S. to impose a ban of its kind when the governor signed the executive order Tuesday.

item.title: Catching up in the AI race? India gets its second AI unicorn in a month
item.description: Two Indian AI startups have become unicorns in a month, which has raised hopes that the country could shed its reputation as an AI laggard.

item.title: Ex-Fed advisor gets over three years in prison for lying about China ties
item.description: The case marks one of the most prominent U.S. prosecutions alleging Chinese intelligence targeting of U.S. institutions, as Trump intensifies its pursuit of foreign economic espionage.

item.title: Munis are having a solid 2026. Investors can still grab 'generationally attractive yields'
item.description: Professionals still see opportunities ahead for municipal bond investors.

item.title: Foreign investors sweeten on Indian government bonds as equities see a sell-off
item.description: The Indian government bond market is drawing interest from foreign investors as it readies for a likely inclusion in Bloomberg index.

item.title: J&J falls despite a beat-and-raise quarter. Why we're raising our price target anyways
item.description: Johnson & Johnson's quarter was imperfect, but enough is going right to validate our ownership of the stock.

item.title: Alibaba and Baidu shares jump in Hong Kong on Apple AI partnership
item.description: The technological rivalry between China and the U.S. has intensified, as they race for AI dominance.

item.title: I've opened 70+ credit cards. This is the one I can't stop recommending
item.description: It's a bit cliché at this point, but there's a reason the Chase Sapphire Preferred is such a great travel credit card.

item.title: Warren Buffett tells CNBC he initiated Berkshire Hathaway's investment in Alphabet
item.description: Warren Buffett said he was the driving force behind the conglomerate's recent big investment in Alphabet.

item.title: Bank of Korea raises rates to 2.75% in first hike in over three years
item.description: The 25 basis point hike by the Bank of Korea was in line with a survey of economists polled by Reuters.

item.title: Mortgage rates rise to highest level in nearly a year, causing homebuyers to pause
item.description: Mortgage rates moved higher last week, causing buyers to pull back, but refinancing did see small gains.

item.title: SpaceX stock sinks below $135 IPO price for the first time
item.description: SpaceX shares fell for a fourth-straight session on Wednesday days after entering the Nasdaq-100

item.title: Kalshi traders see gas prices crossing $4 by end of July
item.description: Like last month's average, Kalshi traders see gas prices surpassing $4 by the end of July.

item.title: Warren Buffett on the market today: 'It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling'
item.description: The investor has been sharply critical of the stock market, one that he says is increasingly defined by speculative trading as opposed to long-term investing.

item.title: Historic IBM stock crash sets up unique options strategy
item.description: IBM shares fell just over $73 to ~$217 — a jaw-dropping 25% single-day decapitation.

item.title: Buying stock with borrowed money reaches level of past market tops: 'Very bearish looking'
item.description: The percentage of margin debt is as high as at prior market tops, according to data from The Leuthold Group.
